# Demo Evidence Report — STORY-120
## TerminalReporter FindingsRender Enum Migration (v0.9.0, issue #62)

**Date:** 2026-06-18
**Worktree:** `.worktrees/STORY-120`
**Binary version:** `wirerust 0.9.0`
**Fixture:** `tests/fixtures/local-samples/modbus-large.pcap` (85-packet, 5-flow Modbus TCP corpus; generated by `mk_modbus_large_pcap.py`)
**Test gate:** `cargo test --all-targets` — 408 tests, 0 failed, 0 regressions

---

## Coverage Map

| Recording | Variant | CLI Flag | AC | Evidence |
|-----------|---------|----------|----|----------|
| AC-003-flat-collapsed.{gif,webm} | `FindingsRender::FlatCollapsed` | (none — default) | AC-003, AC-013 | 12 findings collapsed to groups; 34 identical writes shown as `(x34)` |
| AC-003-grouped.{gif,webm} | `FindingsRender::Grouped` | `--mitre` | AC-003, AC-012 | Tactic headers (`## Discovery`, `## Inhibit Response Function`, `## Impair Process Control`) appear; em-dash + technique names present; no `(xN)` suffix |
| AC-003-flat-expanded.{gif,webm} | `FindingsRender::FlatExpanded` | `--no-collapse` | AC-003, AC-014 | One finding header per raw finding; 34 writes appear as 34 individual lines; no `(xN)` suffix |

---

## Mode-by-Mode Summary

### AC-003-flat-collapsed — FindingsRender::FlatCollapsed (default)

Command: `wirerust --no-color analyze --modbus --reassemble <pcap>`

The FINDINGS section dispatch reaches the `FindingsRender::FlatCollapsed` match arm.
Repeated findings are collapsed: the 34 identical `FC 0x10` writes collapse to one group
`(x34)`, the 2 recon findings to `(x2)`, etc. This is byte-identical to pre-refactor
behavior (STORY-118 established the collapse pass; STORY-120 only changes the dispatch
mechanism from `if self.collapse_findings` to `match self.render`).

Key observable: `(x34)` suffix visible on the write-command finding header.

### AC-003-grouped — FindingsRender::Grouped (--mitre)

Command: `wirerust --no-color analyze --modbus --reassemble --mitre <pcap>`

The FINDINGS section dispatch reaches the `FindingsRender::Grouped` match arm.
The `render_findings_grouped` function is called, which groups findings by MITRE tactic:
- `## Discovery` — 5 recon findings (T0888)
- `## Inhibit Response Function` — 2 DoS findings (T0814)
- `## Impair Process Control` — 39 write/burst findings (T1692.001, T0806, T0835, T0836, T0831)

Each finding line carries `MITRE: <id> — <technique name>` (em-dash + name from ADR-0003 / BC-2.11.016).
No `(xN)` suffix appears anywhere — the collapse pass is not reachable from the `Grouped` arm (AC-012).

Key observables: tactic section headers appear; em-dash names; no collapse suffixes.

### AC-003-flat-expanded — FindingsRender::FlatExpanded (--no-collapse)

Command: `wirerust --no-color analyze --modbus --reassemble --no-collapse <pcap>`

The FINDINGS section dispatch reaches the `FindingsRender::FlatExpanded` match arm.
`render_finding_flat` is called directly for each raw finding. The 34 `FC 0x10` write
commands appear as 34 separate individual headers, each with their own evidence line.
MITRE IDs are shown as `MITRE: T1692.001, T0836` (no em-dash, flat format per BC-2.11.017).
No `(xN)` suffix appears anywhere.

Key observables: distinct TxnID values per line (0x0100 through 0x011F); one header per finding; no collapse.

---

## Structural Verification

The `FindingsRender` enum is defined at `src/reporter/terminal.rs:101`:
```rust
pub enum FindingsRender {
    Grouped,        // --mitre flag
    FlatCollapsed,  // default (v0.8.0+)
    FlatExpanded,   // --no-collapse flag
}
```

The dispatch at `src/reporter/terminal.rs:203–216` is an exhaustive `match self.render`
replacing the pre-v0.9.0 `if self.show_mitre_grouping { ... } else if self.collapse_findings { ... } else { ... }` chain.

The impossible state `show_mitre_grouping = true && collapse_findings = true` is structurally
unrepresentable — no `FindingsRender` variant encodes both simultaneously.

---
